entry.title: Washington Post: 19 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: NATO chief says war could last years; battle in Luhansk drags on
  • Explosive mines bring ‘dangerous beach season’ to Ukraine
  • Macron faces critical test as French parliamentary election gets underway
  • In Colombia’s election, two populists compete to upend status quo
  • ‘Day Zero’ water crisis looms on South Africa’s eastern cape
  • Russia makes ‘marginal gains’ near Severodonetsk
  • Dom Phillips, journalist who chronicled Amazon deforestation, is dead at 57
  • Bruno Pereira, expert on Brazil’s Indigenous communities, dead at 41
  • Syrian leader receives credentials from Bahrain ambassador
  • Spain, Germany battle wildfires amid unusual heat wave
  • Tight Colombian runoff pits former rebel, millionaire
  • Israeli forces kill Palestinian near separation barrier
  • Officials: Gunmen kill 2 police in SW Pakistan province
  • IS claims attack on Sikh temple in Afghan capital of Kabul
  • Pakistan frees 20 Indian fishermen, dozens still in prison
  • Camino pilgrims help rural Spain’s emptying villages survive
  • Russia-West tensions inflame UN debate on Mali peacekeepers
  • Celia, Blas weaken, but still bring heavy rain, surf
  • Modern ‘redlining’ is pushing some Texans out of their homes
  • Yellowstone to partly reopen after historic flooding
  • Local and state Democrats look to ban gun sales near schools
  • Biden takes a spill on a morning bike ride at the beach
  • Fed’s interest rate hikes may mark start of tough new economic climate
  • Peter Thiel helped build big tech. Now he wants to tear it all down.
  • The Fed Hasn’t Fixed Its Worst Blunder Since the 1970s
  • Biden Is Delivering on His Bipartisan Promise
  • Populism Isn’t the Answer to Colombia’s Problems
  • A Conservative Plan to Strengthen Families, Post-Roe
  • Here’s what voters will get if they cast their ballots based on gas prices
  • Texas AG Paxton’s shameful Uvalde cop-out: ‘God always has a plan’
  • Thanks, Kevin McCarthy, for making the Jan. 6 hearings worthwhile
  • The baby formula crisis isn’t over. Key problems remain.
  • Fix the electoral count law now, before Trump tries to exploit it again
  • Afghan families in the U.S., severed and in pain
  • We need a plan to deny the election deniers victory
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Send a letter to the editor
  • Contact the reader representative
  • Submit an op-ed
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: Guardian: 19 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • England v Barbarians: rugby union – live!

  • Russia-Ukraine war: Moscow ‘trying to make Kharkiv a frontline city’, Ukraine warns, as attacks intensify – live

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 19 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 19 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-19 14:15PM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Resuming quarantine-free travel with mainland China to be top priority, Hong Kong’s incoming health minister says

  • Australia to get US-made nuclear submarines by 2030, opposition leader claims

  • Malaysia wants to fight inflation with GST. Will it cost Ismail Sabri at the polls?

  • Will Indonesia’s Jokowi start his own political party to cement his legacy?

  • Kelly Holmes, 52, British Olympic athletics champion, comes out as gay after ‘years of heartache’

  • Joe Biden takes a spill while dismounting after bike ride near the US president’s holiday home

  • Spain battles to bring fires under control as it swelters in heatwave
  • Defrocked Chilean priest sentenced to 15 years in prison for sexually abusing minors
  • Ukraine plans to resume peace talks with Russia by August, officials say
  • Coronavirus: vaccine roll-out for American children under 5 close after CDC panel vote
  • ‘Bloodsicles’ and ice baths: How US zookeepers coax animals through heatwaves
  • Brazil police arrest third suspect in Amazon shooting deaths
  • Kate Bush classic Running Up That Hill tops UK chart after 37 years
  • Gunman, 70, kills 3 senior citizens during potluck dinner at Alabama church
  • Nearly all of Wall Street – and the Fed – botched calls for 2022 as stocks, Treasuries, bitcoin slump in tandem
  • Climate change: China’s state firms take lead in advocating carbon neutrality, must do more about own emissions, experts say
  • Climate-change: BlackRock votes against fewer directors as more companies adopt TCFD framework for risk disclosures, panel hears
  • What makes the e-CNY different from bitcoin? Central bank digital currencies share little with cryptocurrencies
  • Bitcoin’s cascading losses accelerate in record-breaking rout
  • Cities through a pilot’s eyes – folksy and scholarly musings on urban landscapes seen fleetingly
  • Hailey Bieber launches a celebrity skincare brand, Rhode, and Kim Kardashian’s Skkn by Kim is coming right up. They join a crowded field
  • How to age well: mature dad shed 15kg in 6 months to keep up with his toddler son; follow this newly fit doctor’s tips to lose weight
  • Thailand, land of smiles ... and chocolate. Home-grown cacao beans and locally made bars embraced in Bangkok
  • Meeting Muhammad Ali, tussling with a tennis legend, living easy on Lamma – Nick Lovatt the bearded book man on his 70 years in Hong Kong
  • 6 Hong Kong heritage and historical buildings that dodged demolition, and 6 that did not, since 1997
  • Like Queen Elizabeth, China’s Kangxi Emperor had a long and glorious reign; he did start younger than her, though, at the age of seven
  • When Hong Kong was a hotbed of high-speed smuggling and nautical shoot-outs, dubbed ‘Chicago-on-the-Sea’ as public alarm grew

  • Maggie Cheung in her own words, on Wong Kar-wai and In the Mood for Love, marrying Olivier Assayas and being an actress, not just a film star
  • Who is bitcoin billionaire Michael Saylor? The MicroStrategy CEO’s net worth once dropped by US$6 billion in one day during the dot-com boom … will the same happen with his crypto investments?
  • Who are the richest Fast & Furious stars? Net worths, ranked – from Dwayne ‘The Rock’ Johnson and Vin Diesel, to Wonder Woman’s Gal Gadot and the late Paul Walker, here’s who raked in millions
  • Meet Elon Musk’s sister Tosca, founder of Netflix-style streaming service Passionflix, accomplished filmmaker and mother of twins – but did her brother invest in her company?
  • 13 little-known facts about Lightyear star Chris Evans, from crushing on Sandra Bullock and matching Avengers tattoos with Scarlett Johansson, to starring in a Marilyn Manson music video
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

The Korea JoongAng Daily is an English-language daily published by the JoongAng Group, Korea’s leading media group, in association with The New York Times.

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 19 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Strong quake rattles the Noto Peninsula and nearby areas
  • 6,000 yen per adult eyed for ticket to Expo 2025 in Osaka
  • Avid air mile fans fly to other end of Japan for quick break, head home
  • A-bomb victims air views before 1st meeting of U.N. nuclear ban pact
  • Rating system to start in Osaka for private ‘minpaku’ tourist facilities
  • N. Korea deploys medical teams to battle intestinal outbreak
  • Apple workers vote to unionize at Maryland store
  • NATO warns of long Ukraine war as EU bolsters support for Kyiv
  • In Ukraine, funeral for activist killed and mourned in war
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
